description: Upgrade ReAgent from a tool-using research agent into a research-first cognition kernel with structured neuron state, multi-hypothesis reasoning, cognition-driven actions, and operator-visible cognition inspection across CLI, API, and Web.

userStories.title: Define a structured neuron node model
userStories.description: As a system developer, I want the agent's cognition state to be represented as structured neuron nodes so that the runtime can support weighting, conflicts, decay, and action decisions instead of flat string arrays.
acceptanceCriteria: Define one canonical neuron node shape
Each neuron node includes `id`, `kind`, `content`, `salience`, `confidence`, `source`, and `updatedAt`
`kind` supports at least `perception`, `memory`, `hypothesis`, `reasoning`, `action`, and `reflection`
Session persistence remains backward-compatible with older digest-only session data
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in runtime digest persistence and backward-compatible neuron parsing.

userStories.title: Persist cognition state alongside the legacy session digest
userStories.description: As an operator, I want cognition state to persist across turns and process restarts so that the agent keeps a stable internal model instead of rebuilding everything from scratch each time.
acceptanceCriteria: Session store persists cognition state alongside the existing digest summary
Legacy `recentUserIntents`, `recentToolOutcomes`, and `pendingActions` are still available for compatibility
Cognition state can be initialized from old digest-only sessions and recent turns
Persisted sessions remain readable after the migration
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed with structured neuron persistence layered under the legacy digest summaries.

userStories.title: Align cognition memory with workspace memory and artifacts
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want memory neurons to explicitly distinguish workspace memory, session history, tool results, and artifact-derived context so that short-term cognition and long-term knowledge do not get mixed together.
acceptanceCriteria: Memory neurons record whether they came from workspace memory, session history, tool results, or artifacts
Workspace memory recall hits can be converted into cognition nodes
Artifact-backed context can be converted into cognition nodes with source metadata
The runtime does not misclassify long-term memory as temporary hypothesis state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed with workspace-memory and artifact-memory provenance carried into memory neurons and covered by runtime tests.

userStories.title: Support multiple hypotheses instead of a single implicit conclusion
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the agent to maintain multiple competing hypotheses so that it does not converge too early on one unsupported conclusion.
acceptanceCriteria: Cognition state supports multiple hypothesis nodes at once
Each hypothesis can record supporting and conflicting evidence references or summaries
Each hypothesis records a confidence value
When evidence is weak, the runtime keeps hypotheses provisional instead of presenting them as final conclusions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed with multi-hypothesis runtime generation, explicit hypothesis status, and supporting/conflicting evidence summaries.

userStories.title: Add neuron activation, decay, and conflict updates
userStories.description: As a system developer, I want cognition nodes to activate, decay, and react to conflicting evidence so that the kernel behaves more like an evolving research judgment process than a static note list.
acceptanceCriteria: New input can increase relevant node salience
Stale nodes can decay over time or over turns
New evidence can increase or decrease node confidence
Conflicting evidence can reduce hypothesis confidence or mark explicit conflict
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed with layer reconciliation, stale-node decay, and conflict-aware confidence adjustments for repeated and competing hypotheses.

userStories.title: Inject structured neuron summaries into prompts
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the model to see a structured cognition summary instead of scattered context fragments so that responses and actions become more stable across turns.
acceptanceCriteria: Prompt input includes a structured neuron or cognition block
The cognition block covers perception, memory, hypothesis, reasoning, and action
The injected block does not expose raw private chain-of-thought
The cognition block coexists with memory primer and turn history instead of replacing them completely
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed with structured neuron summaries injected alongside memory primer and recent session history.

userStories.title: Generate a reflection layer after each completed turn
userStories.description: As a system developer, I want each turn to leave a minimal reflection so that the next turn knows what was confirmed, what remains uncertain, and what should happen next.
acceptanceCriteria: Each completed turn updates a reflection node or equivalent reflection summary
Reflection covers at least what was confirmed, what remains uncertain, and what next action is recommended
Reflection can update or reinforce action and reasoning state for the next turn
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed with richer reflection nodes that summarize confirmation, unresolved uncertainty, and the recommended next action.

userStories.title: Drive tool choice from cognition state
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want tool selection to be driven by the agent's current cognition state so that it behaves like a research workflow rather than random tool invocation.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool choice reads current action, hypothesis, and reasoning state
High uncertainty can bias the runtime toward evidence-gathering actions instead of premature synthesis
High-confidence conclusions can bias the runtime toward delivery or reuse actions
Existing tool policy can still block disallowed actions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Drive bounded delegations from cognition state
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `search`, `reading`, and `synthesis` delegations to align with the current cognition state so that delegation becomes a reasoned action instead of a prompt-level add-on.
acceptanceCriteria: Delegation creation can read action and hypothesis state
Bounded `search`, `reading`, and `synthesis` delegations align with the current cognition state
Delegation results can write back into cognition state
Delegation behavior still respects the existing bounded guardrails
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expose cognition inspection in the root CLI
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a root CLI cognition view so that I can inspect what the agent currently perceives, remembers, hypothesizes, reasons about, and plans to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `reagent agent cognition <sessionId|senderId>`
CLI cognition output covers perception, memory, hypothesis, reasoning, action, and reflection
CLI cognition output supports `--json`
CLI cognition output does not expose raw private chain-of-thought
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed with `reagent agent cognition`, structured text output, and JSON output over the canonical runtime surface.

userStories.title: Expose cognition inspection in the HTTP API
userStories.description: As a Web console or integration developer, I want a cognition API so that higher-level surfaces can render the current cognition state without reimplementing runtime logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `/api/agent/sessions/:sessionId/cognition`
The API returns the same cognition model used by the CLI
The API does not expose raw private chain-of-thought
Existing session, profile, history, and hooks routes remain compatible
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed with `/api/agent/sessions/:sessionId/cognition` returning the structured cognition model used by the CLI.

userStories.title: Add a cognition panel to the Web Agents surface
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the Web `Agents` panel to show the current cognition state so that I can quickly understand what the agent is focusing on, what hypotheses it holds, and why it is choosing the next action.
acceptanceCriteria: The Web `Agents` panel includes a cognition section
The cognition section shows at least perception, hypothesis, reasoning, and action state
The cognition section supports empty, weak, and conflict states with readable UI copy
The Web cognition section does not expose raw private chain-of-thought
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed with a new Web Agents cognition tab that renders empty, provisional, and conflict-heavy cognition layers.
